title: Unable to assign the IPs
body:
/kind bug


**Description**
I am testing an application and trying to assign an IP to a container but it kept on failing with the following messages:
```
# podman create \
   --hostname test1 \
   --name test1 \
   --net test_nw \
   --ip 172.16.1.90 \
   oraclelinux:7
Error: error running container create option: cannot set a static IP if
joining additional CNI networks: invalid argument
```

I am using the following network config:
```
# cat /etc/cni/net.d/88-racpub-bridge.conflist
{
    "cniVersion": "0.3.0",
    "name": "test_nw",
    "plugins": [
      {
        "type": "bridge",
        "bridge": "cni0",
        "isGateway": true,
        "ipMasq": true,
        "ipam": {
            "type": "host-local",
            "subnet": "172.16.1.0/24",
            "routes": [
                { "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }
            ]
        }
      },
      {
        "type": "portmap",
        "capabilities": {
          "portMappings": true
        }
      }
    ]
}
```

**Describe the results you received:**

It throws the following error:
```
Error: error running container create option: cannot set a static IP if
joining additional CNI networks: invalid argument
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
IP should have got assigned to the container.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.9
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-4.0.1.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"ol"'
    version: "7.8"
  MemFree: 13915369472
  MemTotal: 16535801856
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 8589131776
  SwapTotal: 8589930496
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: raconpodman
  kernel: 4.14.35-1902.305.4.el7uek.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 73h 54m 19.03s (Approximately 3.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - container-registry.oracle.com

  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf

  GraphDriverName: overlay

  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.4.4-4.0.1.el7.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Virtual Machines

comments.body: This was fixed in Podman 1.7.0, consider upgrading.
